outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Rubio announces 'significant progress' in Iran talks from New Delhi as deal announcement looms
outlets.summary: Secretary Rubio says 'significant progress' in Iran negotiations from New Delhi, hinting world could get 'good news' within hours. Axios exclusive reveals 60-day MOU framework: Hormuz Phase 1, nuclear Phase 2.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Pezeshkian says Iran 'ready to assure world' it doesn't seek nuclear weapons as deal talks intensify
outlets.summary: Iranian president tells IRIB state TV Iran not seeking nukes, calls Israel the destabilizing actor. Simultaneously, Netanyahu calls emerging deal 'very bad' for Israel. Rubio: 'significant progress.'

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: US-Iran deal talks: 'Significant progress' — but Netanyahu says terms 'very bad' for Israel
outlets.summary: Rubio announces significant progress from New Delhi, White House hopes for Sunday announcement. Netanyahu convenes emergency meeting, calls deal 'very bad' — nuclear deferred, missiles off table, Lebanon clause added.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Netanyahu calls Iran deal 'very bad' for Israel — nuclear deferred, missiles untouched; GOP hawks warn Trump
outlets.summary: Netanyahu convenes emergency meeting on deal terms his coalition calls 'very bad' — nuclear file deferred, ballistic missiles excluded. Cruz, Wicker, Graham warn Trump against signing. Netanyahu reportedly urged resuming strikes.

outlets.name: Washington Times
outlets.headline: Iran deal imminent — but Israel says it's shut out, nuclear left for 'later'
outlets.summary: Washington Times exclusive: US-Iran peace deal announcement expected within 24 hours. Deal defers nuclear issue to 60-day follow-on talks. Israel excluded from negotiations, calling terms 'very bad.'

outlets.name: The Hill
outlets.headline: Senate hawks blast Iran deal: Wicker 'disaster,' Cruz 'disastrous mistake,' Graham 'pour gasoline'
outlets.summary: SASC Chairman Wicker: 60-day ceasefire is 'a disaster' that negates Operation Epic Fury. Cruz: deal with Iran enriching uranium would be 'disastrous mistake.' Graham: deal would 'pour gasoline on conflicts in Lebanon and Iraq.'

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Rubio: 'Significant progress' in Iran talks — what to know about the emerging deal
outlets.summary: Rubio from New Delhi says 'significant progress,' hints at 'good news' within hours. Axios reveals 60-day MOU: Hormuz opens (Phase 1), nuclear talks follow (Phase 2). Iran FM: positions have 'moved closer.'

outlets.name: BBC News
outlets.headline: Iran-US deal close as Rubio signals 'good news' — but Israel and Iran hawks remain obstacles
outlets.summary: US Secretary of State Rubio says 'significant progress' from New Delhi. Netanyahu convenes emergency meeting calling terms 'very bad.' Iran's Tasnim says Hormuz will remain under Iran's management.

outlets.name: PBS NewsHour
outlets.headline: Starmer and Macron welcome Hormuz deal progress — but insist freedom of navigation must be permanent
outlets.summary: UK PM Starmer and French President Macron welcome progress but insist on 'unconditional and unrestricted' Hormuz navigation. UK and France jointly planning international maritime security mission.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Netanyahu calls emerging US-Iran deal 'very bad' — convenes emergency meeting, urges Trump to resume strikes
outlets.summary: Netanyahu holds emergency coalition-security council meeting after Channel 12 reports deal terms 'very bad': nuclear deferred, missiles off table, Lebanon clause, Iran keeps Hormuz control. Netanyahu reportedly urged Trump to resume strikes.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: Trump: 'Netanyahu will do whatever I want' — as Israel sounds alarm on deal terms
outlets.summary: Trump told reporters Netanyahu 'will do whatever I want him to do — he's a very good man,' dismissing Israel's concerns. Netanyahu responded by convening emergency meeting and urging resumed strikes.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Israel sidelined, outmaneuvered as Trump-Iran deal takes shape without Jerusalem
outlets.summary: Analysis: Israel excluded from US-Iran negotiations. Netanyahu's objections — nuclear deferral, missiles off table, Lebanon clause — reflect a fundamental loss of Israeli leverage over the war's endgame.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Pezeshkian: Iran ready to assure the world it seeks no nuclear weapons — Hormuz stays under Iran management
outlets.summary: President Pezeshkian tells IRIB Iran does not seek nuclear weapons. Tasnim and Fars news agencies simultaneously push back: nuclear file is NOT in MOU, Hormuz will remain under Iran's management regardless of deal.

outlets.name: IRNA
outlets.headline: Iran FM spokesman: positions have moved closer; nuclear issue to be handled separately from war-ending deal
outlets.summary: Foreign Ministry spokesman Baghaei says US-Iran positions have 'moved closer in recent days.' Confirms nuclear file is NOT part of current war-ending negotiations — Tehran seeks to end the war first.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Tasnim: Iran has NOT agreed to nuclear measures — Hormuz will NOT return to pre-war status
outlets.summary: Tasnim (IRGC-linked): Iran has not agreed to any new nuclear measures. Strait of Hormuz will 'not return to its pre-war status.' If US naval blockade on Iranian ports is not lifted, 'no changes will be made in the Strait.'
